question_text: A balanced seesaw has a child of mass 30 kg sitting 1.5 m from the pivot on the left side. Another child sits on the right side, 1 m from the pivot. Calculate the mass of the child on the right side. (Use g = 10 N/kg)
explanation: For a balanced seesaw, clockwise moments = anticlockwise moments. Left side: force = 30 × 10 = 300 N; moment = 300 × 1.5 = 450 N·m. Right side: moment must also be 450 N·m. If distance = 1 m, then force = 450 ÷ 1 = 450 N. Since force = mass × 10, mass = 450 ÷ 10 = 45 kg.
